claim: The increased toughness in MHT results from higher crack propagation energy, derived from the combined effect of reducing intergranular precipitates and a high fraction of special grain boundaries.
evidence_text: the increase in toughness is due to the rise in the crack propagation energy. The increased toughness is derived from the combined effect of reducing the number of intergranular precipitates and a high percentage of “special grain boundaries”.

claim: Special grain boundaries (3 < Σ ≤ 29) have lower interfacial energy, low excess free volumes, and better resistance to grain boundary damage compared to random high-angle grain boundaries.
evidence_text: low-energy grain boundaries (so called “special” boundaries, such as annealing twins and their variants) provide better interfacial properties than random grain boundaries with high-angle (RHAGBs), which is reflected in the lower interfacial energy and resistance to grain boundary damage [31,32]. “Special” grain boundaries are characterized by a particular misorientation, low excess free volumes, and a high degree of atomic matching. They are described geometrically by a low “sigma number, ∑ ( 3 < ∑ ≤ 29 )

claim: TEM reveals κ-carbide particles with sizes of 3–10 nm that are co-gridded with austenite and have an orientation relationship [011]γ // [011]κ.
evidence_text: dark-field (DF) TEM images corresponding to the additional diffraction spots show κ-carbide particles with sizes ranging from 3 to 10 nm (Fig. 3 (a–e, i) marked by the white arrow (point). Moreover, ... κ-carbide that is co-gridded with austenite. In addition, these κ-carbides have a certain orientation relationship with the austenite, that is, $[ 0 1 1 ] _ { \gamma } / / [ 0 1 1 ] _ { \kappa } .$

claim: TEM confirms that the elongated grain-boundary precipitates in S1000 are Cr7C3.
evidence_text: the elongated stripe-like precipitates on the austenite grain boundaries of S1000 are $\mathrm { C r } _ { 7 } \mathrm { C } _ { 3 }$ , as shown in Fig. 3(c and d).

claim: SAED patterns of austenite show additional faint superlattice diffraction spots, indicating the presence of κ-carbide co-gridded with austenite with orientation relationship [011]γ // [011]κ.
evidence_text: The selected area diffraction (SAED) patterns of austenite show additional faint superlattice diffraction spots ... This indicates the presence of κ-carbide that is co-gridded with austenite. In addition, these κ-carbides have a certain orientation relationship with the austenite, that is, $[ 0 1 1 ] _ { \gamma } / / [ 0 1 1 ] _ { \kappa } .$

claim: Elongated stripe-like precipitates on the austenite grain boundaries of S1000 are identified as Cr7C3.
evidence_text: the elongated stripe-like precipitates on the austenite grain boundaries of S1000 are Cr7C3, as shown in Fig. 3(c and d).

claim: Cr7C3 precipitates on grain boundaries dissolve when the temperature is increased to 1050°C in S1050.
evidence_text: As the temperature increased to 1050 °C, Cr7C3 dissolves on the grain boundary.

claim: S1000 exhibits grain size (deff) 3.17 µm, yield strength 955 ± 7 MPa, ultimate tensile strength 1126 ± 3 MPa, total elongation 55.5 ± 1.3%, and impact energy 29.2 ± 2.0 J.
S1050 has grain size 3.84 µm, YS 945 ± 14 MPa, UTS 1113 ± 3 MPa, total elongation 55.8 ± 0.4%, and impact energy 42.8 ± 0.4 J.
MHT has grain size 7.31 µm, YS 910 ± 7 MPa, UTS 1076 ± 2 MPa, total elongation 50.9 ± 3.0%, and impact energy 55.1 ± 2.2 J.
As effective grain size increases from 3.17 µm (S1000) to 3.84 µm (S1050), YS and UTS decrease by 10 MPa, while total elongation remains similar.
evidence_text: The d_eff increase from 3.17 μm in S1000 to 3.84 μm in S1050 and the YS and UTS concomitantly decrease by 10 MPa, whereas the TEL is similar for both samples.

claim: MHT with larger deff of 7.31 µm shows YS, UTS, and TEL declined by 35 MPa, 37 MPa, and 4.6% respectively compared to S1050 at 25°C.
evidence_text: compared with S1050 with a d_eff of 3.84 μm, MHT with a larger d_eff of 7.31 μm has slighter lower YS, UTS, and TEL values (declined by 35, 37, and 4.6 %, respectively) at 25°C

claim: Samples quenched after holding at 1100°C for 3 min (S1100) exhibit a yield strength of 827 MPa and tensile strength of 998 MPa.
evidence_text: The yield strength of the samples held at 1100 °C for 3 min decrease to 827 MPa and the tensile strength decrease to 998 MPa.

claim: The difference in impact energy among the three specimens is mainly due to crack propagation energy, which is 9.87 J (S1000), 26.64 J (S1050), and 36.78 J (MHT), with corresponding displacements of 4.55, 6.30, and 8.04 mm.
evidence_text: The difference in the impact energy of the three specimens is mainly due to the difference in the crack propagation energy, which increases from 9.87 J for S1000 to 26.64 J for S1050 and finally to 36.78 J for MHT. The displacements of S1000, S1050, and MHT are 4.55, 6.30, and 8.04 mm, respectively.

claim: All steels show non-uniform dimples in tensile fracture; Cr7C3 on grain boundaries in S1000 and B2 in S1050/MHT do not significantly decrease ductility; MHT exhibits underdeveloped dimples and slightly reduced plasticity.
evidence_text: All steels show dimples, but the dimples are not uniform in size. ... Cr C on the grain boundaries of S1000 and B2 in the grains of S1050 and MHT does not significantly decrease the ductility of the steels. Underdeveloped dimples are present in MHT, and the plasticity is also slightly reduced.

claim: Shear zone area increases from S1000 to MHT; S1000 and S1050 show quasicleavage planes, slip bands, microcracks, and microvoids, while S1000 exhibits intergranular fracture; MHT shows ductile fracture with increased dimples and no quasi-cleavage planes.
evidence_text: The low-magnification fractograms ... show that the shear zone areas of S1000, S1050, and MHT gradually increase. ... The locally magnified micrographs ... show quasicleavage planes in S1000 and S1050 and slip bands on the plane. ... Microcracks and microvoids are observed ... The grain boundaries of S1000 are also apparent ... which is a characteristic of intergranular fractures. In the MHT sample, the dimples increase and microcracks disappear, but no quasi-cleavage plane, which is a ductile fracture, is observed.

claim: The austenite of S1000 contains 20.97±0.34 wt% Mn, 9.30±0.02 wt% Al, 2.04±0.26 wt% Ni, 3.24±0.78 wt% Cr, 1.17±0.13 wt% C, and balance Fe.
evidence_text: Table 2 Chemical composition of austenite in as-heat treatment specimens. ... S1000 20.97 ± 0.34 9.30 ± 0.02 2.04 ± 0.26 3.24 ± 0.78 1.17 ± 0.13 Bal.

claim: The austenite of S1050 contains 21.09±0.86 wt% Mn, 9.00±0.11 wt% Al, 2.19±0.16 wt% Ni, 3.16±0.17 wt% Cr, 1.22±0.03 wt% C, and balance Fe.
The austenite of MHT contains 20.76±0.61 wt% Mn, 9.46±0.33 wt% Al, 2.01±0.25 wt% Ni, 3.10±0.57 wt% Cr, 1.15±0.07 wt% C, and balance Fe.

The grain boundary strengthening σGR follows the Hall-Petch relationship σGR = K deff^(-1/2) with K = 461 MPa μm^1/2, and the values for S1000, S1050, and MHT are 145 MPa, 120 MPa, and 63 MPa, respectively.
evidence_text: σGR is the grain boundary strengthening following the Hall-Petch relationship, calculated as σGR = K deff^{-1/2}; K is the slope of the Hall-Petch plot (461 MPa μm^{1/2}) ... The grain-boundary strengths of S1000, S1050, and MHT are 145, 120, and 63 MPa, respectively, which are slightly larger gap.

claim: The precipitation strengthening contribution σPPT, calculated as σYS - σ0 - σGR, is 209 MPa for S1000, 216 MPa for S1050, and 248 MPa for MHT, and the differences are not significant.
evidence_text: σYS - σ0 - σGR is used to calculate σ. The calculated values for S1000, S1050, and MHT are 209, 216, and 248 MPa, respectively, which are not significantly different.

claim: In S1000, the presence of Cr7C3 at grain boundaries leads to preferential crack nucleation at grain boundaries, and cracks generally follow random high-angle grain boundaries (RHAGBs) under further deformation, with special boundaries having limited effect on changing crack propagation or deflection.
evidence_text: the presence of Cr7C3 at the grain boundaries in S1000 led to preferential nucleation of the cracks at the grain boundaries... the cracks generally follow the RHAGBs under further deformation extension. The special boundaries in S1000 have limited effect on changing the crack propagation or deflection.

claim: In S1050, with intergranular precipitates eliminated, special boundaries interfere with RHAGBs, inhibiting intergranular crack propagation; cracks propagate along special boundaries or are deflected, producing a hybrid fracture mode of intergranular and transgranular fracture.
evidence_text: when the intergranular precipitates are eliminated in S1050, the grains grow and the special boundaries interfere with the RHAGBs; thus, further propagation of the intergranular cracks is inhibited, and the cracks propagate along the special boundaries (shown by the yellow dashed lines in Fig. 8(c) or are deflected (Fig. 8(d) shown by white arrows), producing ductile fracture in the form of a hybrid fracture mode with intergranular and transgranular fracture.

claim: In MHT, a larger number of special boundaries and larger grain size cause greater impediment to dislocations, leading crack paths to constantly cross special boundaries with constant deflection and propagation, increasing effective crack propagation length and energy dissipation, which increases impact toughness by 29%.
evidence_text: Compared with S1050, MHT has a larger number of specific boundaries and larger grain size, which impedes dislocations emanating from the crack tip to a greater extent. ... the crack paths in MHT constantly cross the special boundaries and are subjected to constant deflection and propagation... This process is exacerbated by larger grain sizes, which increase the effective length of the crack propagation, leading to greater energy dissipation; thus, the impact toughness increases by 29 %.

claim: The intermediate heat treatment at 750°C in MHT resulted in the distribution of many precipitates at grain boundaries, which increased stress in non-twinned regions, leading to a larger number of twin nucleation sites and finally more special boundaries that interfered with RHAGBs, affecting toughness.
evidence_text: The intermediate heat treatment temperature of 750 °C applied to the MHT specimens resulted in the distribution of many precipitates at the grain boundaries, as shown in Fig. 9(a and b). These closely spaced precipitates led to an increase in the stress in the non-twinned regions, leading to a larger number of twin nucleation sites, and finally more special boundaries that interfered with the RHAGBs, thus affecting the toughness.

claim: The impact process divides into crack initiation and crack propagation stages at maximum load Fm; in S1000, cracks propagate rapidly along high-energy random boundaries due to precipitates, while in MHT, with precipitates dissolved and higher special boundary fraction, crack propagation is hindered and paths are deflected along low-energy special boundaries, increasing energy consumption and impact toughness.
evidence_text: The impact process can be divided into crack initiation and crack propagation stages according to the maximum load (Fm). ... For S1000, the cracks propagate rapidly along the random high-angle boundaries with high energy, despite having a high percentage of special boundaries, cracks tend to nucleate at the grain boundaries because of the precipitates at the boundaries. ... However, the descent of the MHT curve slows down significantly in the crack propagation stage. With the disappearance of precipitates at the grain boundary, increasing grain size and frequency of the special boundaries due to multi-stage heat treatment, the crack propagation is hindered by the special boundaries with low energy in the austenite grains.

claim: Charpy impact process is divided into crack initiation and crack propagation stages by the maximum load (F_m), where the area under the load–displacement curve up to the peak is crack initiation energy and after the peak is crack propagation energy.
evidence_text: The impact process can be divided into crack initiation and crack propagation stages according to the maximum load (F_m). The area from the starting point to the loading peak is the crack initiation energy and the area from the loading peak to the fracture is the crack propagation energy.

claim: The S1000 steel (single-stage heat treatment at 1000 °C) has an austenitic microstructure with a grain size of 3.17 μm, 71.7% special grain boundaries, intergranular Cr7C3 precipitates, and nanoscale κ-carbides of 3–10 nm.
evidence_text: The microstructure of the single-stage heat treatment at 1000 °C steel consists of austenite with a grain size of 3.17 μm, 71.7 % special boundaries with low energy, Cr7C3 along the grain boundaries, and nanoscale κ-carbides (3–10 nm).

claim: Increasing the single-stage heat treatment temperature to 1050 °C (S1050) results in dissolution of Cr7C3 precipitates, austenite grain size growth to 3.84 μm, and a decrease of special grain boundary fraction to 68.3%.
evidence_text: When the temperature is increased to 1050 °C, Cr7C3 dissolves, austenite grains grow to 3.84 μm, and the percentage of special boundaries decreases to 68.3 %.

claim: Multi-stage heat treatment (MHT) dissolves intergranular Cr7C3 precipitates while retaining a high fraction of special grain boundaries (71.2%).
evidence_text: The multi-stage heat treatment facilitates the dissolution of the intergranular precipitates Cr7C3 while maintaining a high proportion of special boundaries at 71.2 %.

claim: Fig. 9 presents SEM micrographs of cross-sections of samples after the intermediate heat treatment at 750 °C in the MHT process.
evidence_text: Fig. 9. SEM micrographs of cross-section of samples subjected to (a) intermediate heat treatment at 750 °C in MHT process, and (b) low magnification the localized region of (a).
